subject: alpacas
one_liner: Buy now, pay in four, for animals that own nothing and want the grooming table anyway.
brand_voice_notes: Straight-faced consumer-finance confidence pointed at livestock. A BNPL company named, sincerely, after the verb for defrauding someone. Every sentence treats an alpaca as a rational credit consumer with a filing status and a repayment obligation. Never winks. The absurdity is in the total seriousness: underwriting, autodraft schedules, co-signed liability, a proprietary score. Deadpan, plain, no exclamation points, no cute animal puns beyond the ones a real brand would ship by accident.

hero.sub: Fleece is buy-now-pay-later built for an animal with no income, no thumbs, and a strong opinion about the grooming table. Four interest-free payments, drafted from the herd, whether the alpaca consents or not.

features.title: The Fleece Score
features.blurb: A proprietary creditworthiness rating for a creature that has never held a job. Built from fiber density, gait, and how long the alpaca can hold eye contact before spitting. Updated hourly. Nobody has passed the review for a mortgage yet, but the model is optimistic.

features.title: Pay in Four
features.blurb: Split any purchase into four interest-free payments: hay on the 1st, hay on the 8th, hay on the 15th, and one payment drafted while the alpaca is asleep. Financeable items include mineral licks, dental floats, and a second alpaca.

features.title: Herd Co-Sign
features.blurb: Every alpaca in the paddock co-signs every other alpaca's balance. One missed payment and the whole herd's Fleece Score drops together, as a unit, standing in a line, staring at you. We call this accountability. The alpacas call it nothing, because they cannot.

pricing_tiers.name: Grazer
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the alpaca just getting its financial footing.
perks: Pay in four on purchases up to $40
One (1) Fleece Score refresh per day

pricing_tiers.name: Full Fleece
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the alpaca ready to take on real obligations.
perks: Soft declines delivered as a gentle hum
A dedicated account manager, shared with eleven other alpacas

pricing_tiers.name: Prize Stud
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the show alpaca and its financial future.
perks: Concierge underwriting reviewed by a human who has met an alpaca
A laminated card the alpaca is physically unable to use

testimonials.quote: My alpaca financed a $600 grooming table in four payments. He has never seen the table. He does not know the table exists. His Fleece Score went up 40 points.
testimonials.name: Dale Prewitt
testimonials.title: Owner-Operator, Two Rivers Alpaca Concern (9 head)

testimonials.quote: We onboarded the entire paddock in one afternoon. Nine alpacas, one shared account, zero questions asked, because they are structurally incapable of asking questions.
testimonials.name: Marisol Vantongeren
testimonials.title: Herd Financial Wellness Coordinator

testimonials.quote: I was approved for a pay-in-four plan on a mineral lick and felt, for the first time, that a financial institution had truly seen me.
testimonials.name: Gerald
testimonials.title: Alpaca (Huacaya, 4yo, no fixed address)

demo_screen_concept: A single alpaca's Fleece account dashboard, product-UI not marketing. Left: a large 'Fleece Score' arc gauge (300-850) with a needle and a deadpan status label ('Grazing in good standing'). Center: an active Pay-in-Four schedule for a financed grooming table, four dated payment chips (two Paid, one Due, one Upcoming) with a progress bar. Below it, a 'Finance the herd' catalog of tappable items (hay bale, mineral lick, dental float, a second alpaca) each with a 'Pay in 4' button that opens a modal splitting the price into four dated payments and nudging the Fleece Score gauge up. Right: a 'Herd Co-Sign' panel listing paddock members with tiny scores that move together, plus a bright red 'Simulate a missed payment' button that drops everyone's score at once with a hard shake. All fake, all client-side.

images.rationale: Hero earns a photo: the neo-brutalist composition frames a deadpan studio alpaca portrait in a keyline card, and a real BNPL brand would ship exactly this. Demo is a finance dashboard (Fleece Score gauge, pay-in-4 schedule), so it gets no image. Testimonial headshots: one of the three users is Gerald, an actual alpaca, so he gets a corporate headshot; the two human ranchers keep CSS initials-circles.

items.prompt: Editorial studio photograph of a single Huacaya alpaca, cream-and-fawn fleece, facing camera head-on with a flat deadpan bureaucratic expression like a corporate headshot; head-and-shoulders, centered; seamless pale blush-pink #FBE4EC studio backdrop; soft even frontal lighting; sharp focus on eyes; hard bans on text/letters/numbers/logos/watermarks/UI.

Deadpan corporate headshot of a single Huacaya alpaca, brown-and-cream fleece, calm slightly self-important LinkedIn-executive expression; tight square head-and-shoulders; soft warm-grey #E8DDE1 backdrop; even soft lighting; hard bans on text/letters/numbers/logos/watermarks/UI/clothing.

design_direction.style: Neo-brutalist flat-bold consumer-finance, daylight blush mode. The page is staged like a loud, overconfident buy-now-pay-later brand that has been aimed, with total sincerity, at livestock. A pale blush-pink ground carries flat blocks with thick 3px near-black keylines and hard, un-blurred offset drop shadows (6px 6px 0 ink), so every card, button, and price tag looks like a sticker pressed onto the page. Nothing is soft: no gradients, no glass, no blur, no rounded clay. Corners are lightly rounded (8-10px) the way a real fintech ships them, but the borders stay heavy and the shadows stay hard. One hot bubblegum pink is the brand and is rationed strictly to the single primary CTA per section, the live 'Pay in 4' affordance, the active toggle, and the round 'pre-approved' stamp, and appears almost nowhere else; everything structural is near-black ink on blush. Archivo Black sets the wordmark and headlines in heavy condensed-feeling grotesque caps, sized large and tight; IBM Plex Sans carries plain, slightly clinical body copy the way a compliance footer would. Every figure that pretends to be money (payment amounts, the Fleece Score, tier prices, purchase limits, co-signed balances) is set in IBM Plex Mono with tabular figures so it reads like a statement line, not a marketing number. Motion is hard and mechanical: buttons translate down-right and collapse their offset shadow to zero on press like a physical key, cards nudge up-left and grow their shadow on hover, the Fleece Score needle snaps between values, and everything is gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. Reads like a well-funded, faintly predatory consumer-lending brand that has done zero diligence on whether its borrowers are people.

fonts.body: 'IBM Plex Sans', -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

design_direction.archetype: Conversion-focused consumer-fintech landing, hero-centric: pre-approval hero, three-up feature blocks, three testimonials, three-tier pricing, compliance-footer close.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Hard mechanical key-press: buttons translate +2px/+2px and collapse their hard offset shadow on :active; cards translate -2px/-2px and grow shadow on hover; Fleece Score needle snaps; stamp thunks in on load. All gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Left column: oversized Archivo Black headline 'Your alpaca is pre-approved.', plain Plex Sans subhead, one pink primary CTA button with hard shadow, and a tiny mono 'Representative example: 4 payments of $0.00 hay. 0% APR. Your herd is the collateral.' compliance line. Right column: the hero image (deadpan studio alpaca portrait) framed in a thick ink keyline card with a hard shadow and a pink round 'PRE-APPROVED' stamp overlapping the corner. Blush ground, no gradient.
